name: Multiplicative Homeostatic Scaling with Urgency Threshold
description: An algebraic rule-based agent inspired by homeostatic synaptic scaling (Turrigiano et al., 1998). Instead of using error-proportional control (Formulation 1) or learned Q-values (Formulation 2), this agent maintains a multiplicative gain factor g(t) = s / A_bar(t) that scales behavioral activation — directly mirroring the biological rule where synaptic weights are scaled by the ratio of target to actual firing rate. A smoothed energy average (exponential moving average) provides temporal filtering analogous to firing-rate integration. Additionally, a hysteresis-based urgency mode switches between foraging and resting states, implementing anticipatory regulation (postulate P5): the agent keeps foraging past the low threshold until reaching the high threshold, preventing oscillatory start-stop behavior. This produces qualitatively different dynamics from the PI controller (no integral memory) and the RL agent (no learning).

variables.name: energy
variables.description: Internal energy level of the agent (regulated variable). Decreases each tick, increases on eating.

variables.name: smoothed_energy
variables.description: Exponential moving average of recent energy levels. Provides temporal filtering analogous to averaging firing rates in Turrigiano et al. (1998).

variables.name: homeostatic_gain
variables.description: Multiplicative scaling factor: g = s / (A_bar + epsilon). When A_bar < s, gain > 1 (upscaling). When A_bar > s, gain < 1 (downscaling). Mirrors Turrigiano's w_i * <r*>/<r(t)> rule.

variables.name: urgency
variables.description: Binary urgency mode: 1 = urgent/foraging, 0 = resting/satiated. Switches with hysteresis to prevent oscillation.

parameters.name: energy_from_eating
parameters.source: Tuned to interaction with decay rate

parameters.name: ema_smoothing_constant
parameters.source: Controls memory of recent energy; inspired by firing-rate averaging in Turrigiano et al. (1998)

parameters.name: low_urgency_threshold
parameters.source: 0.6 * s; critical depletion level triggers urgent foraging; Cannon (1932) proportional response

parameters.name: high_satiety_threshold
parameters.source: 0.95 * s; near set point triggers resting; hysteresis band from control engineering

parameters.name: minimum_gain
parameters.source: Prevents complete behavioral shutdown; finite dynamic range per assumptions

parameters.name: maximum_gain
parameters.source: Caps maximum behavioral activation; finite capacity assumption

parameters.name: division_safety
parameters.source: Prevents division by zero in gain computation

rules.description: Energy dynamics: passive decay each tick, gain from eating food
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if (last_action == 'eat' and consumed) else 0
A = clamp(A - d + delta_eat * ate, 0, A_max)

rules.description: Smoothed energy via exponential moving average (temporal filtering of activity)
Multiplicative homeostatic gain: ratio of set point to smoothed energy, mirroring synaptic scaling rule w_i * <r*> / <r(t)>
Urgency mode with hysteresis: switches to foraging when smoothed energy drops below theta_low, switches to resting when it rises above theta_high, maintains current mode in between
Directional utility for movement: gain-weighted inverse distance to nearest food

decision_logic.description: Two-mode behavioral policy driven by multiplicative gain and urgency hysteresis. In resting mode (u=0), the agent stays still unless food happens to be at its position and energy is below set point (opportunistic eating). In foraging mode (u=1), the agent eats if food is present, moves toward nearest food if visible (weighted by gain), or explores randomly. The gain g amplifies action urgency for depleted agents and dampens it for satiated agents.
pseudocode: # Step 1: Update energy from last action
A = clamp(A - d + delta_eat * ate, 0, A_max)
# Step 4: Update urgency mode with hysteresis
food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list]
# Step 6: Decision based on urgency mode
return 'eat'  # Opportunistic feeding below set point
min_dist = min(abs(p_prime[0]-fx)+abs(p_prime[1]-fy) for fx,fy in food_positions)
# No food visible: random exploration in foraging mode

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food'] — list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.ate_food: last_action == 'eat' and perception['last_action_result'] indicates consumption success

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; agent ignores env reward, uses internal homeostatic gain and urgency for decision-making

expected_behaviors.description: Gain increases as energy drops: severely depleted agent has g >> 1
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 30 steps without food → assert g increases toward g_max as A_bar decreases

expected_behaviors.description: Gain decreases as energy rises: well-fed agent has g < 1
Hysteresis prevents oscillation: agent in foraging mode keeps foraging until theta_high is reached
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: start with A_bar = 45 (u=1), feed agent until A_bar = 55 (between thresholds) → assert u still == 1 (foraging continues)

expected_behaviors.description: Agent transitions to resting only after reaching high satiety threshold
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: keep feeding agent until A_bar > 76 → assert u switches to 0

expected_behaviors.description: In resting mode, agent stays still (conserves energy)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set u = 0 with no food at position → assert action == 'stay'

expected_behaviors.description: In resting mode with food at position and energy below set point, agent opportunistically eats
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set u = 0, A = 70 < s, food at position → assert action == 'eat'

expected_behaviors.description: Higher gain produces stronger preference for nearest food (depleted agent is more directed)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: compare move selection with g=3 vs g=1 toward same food → both pick same direction but g=3 has higher utility differential over alternatives

expected_behaviors.description: Smoothed energy (EMA) lags behind actual energy, providing temporal stability
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: instantly set A from 30 to 80 → assert A_bar moves slowly toward 80 over multiple ticks, not instantly

references: Turrigiano, G.G. et al. (1998) — Activity-dependent scaling of quantal amplitude in neocortical neurons — homeostatic synaptic plasticity
Cannon, W.B. (1932) — The Wisdom of the Body — proportional response and negative feedback
Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2011) — A Reinforcement Learning Theory for Homeostatic Regulation — NeurIPS (set point framework)
Gross, F. et al. (2024) — A Functional Approach to Homeostatic Regulation — Biology Direct
NCBI Bookshelf — Neurobiology of Body Fluid Homeostasis (anticipatory/predictive regulation)
Bernard, C. (1865) — Introduction à l'étude de la médecine expérimentale
